rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Receiver Kevin Singers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order: 1. Authorizing Receiver To Return Security Deposits To Tenants After Owner'S Refusal To Do So; And 2. Authorizing Receiver To List And Market Receivership Real Properties For Sale Prior To Foreclosure
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 10, 2020 line 1. Receiver Kevin Singer's Motion For Order: 1. Authorizing Receiver To Return Security Deposits To Tenants After Owner'S Refusal To Do So; And 2. Authorizing Receiver To List And Market Receivership Real Properties For Sale Prior To Foreclosure READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Receiver's Motion for Order Authorizing Receiver to Return Security Deposits and to List the Property for Sale is GRANTED. Receiver is authorized to return security deposits to the departing tenants in accordance with the law. If additional tenants vacate during the receivership, receiver is authorized to return the security deposit in accordance with the law, if the Defendants, within 10 days of notification by the Receiver that the tenant is vacating, do not confirm that Defendants will be returning the deposit. Receiver may initiate the listing effort for the properties subject to this motion no earlier than July 24, 2020. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: XIAOMEI FENG ET AL VS. JIAWEI JIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Plaintiffs Failure To Amend Complaint After Demurrer Was Sustained And Request For Entry Of Judgment Of Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Arasto Farsad In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jul 10, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT JIAWEI JIAN's Motion To Dismiss Action For Plaintiffs Failure To Amend Complaint After Demurrer Was Sustained And Request For Entry Of Judgment Of Dismissal READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Amend is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to file an amended complaint in accordance with the Order sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend and CRC 3.1320(g). The Court notes that Plaintiff filed an unauthorized First Amended Complaint on June 25, 2020 without leave of Court and without filing any opposition to the motion to dismiss providing an explanation for the delay. The Court further finds that the purported First Amended Complaint is dated May 28, 2020. First Amended Complaint is stricken. = (501/CFH)
